radius.$description: Primitive border-radius values for the River Romney design system. Six stops from sharp (0) to pill (9999px). The system leans toward subtle rounding — sharp and near-sharp corners are more common than heavy rounding, consistent with the technical, terminal-adjacent aesthetic. Do not reference these directly in UI code; use semantic tokens when available.

none.$description: Sharp corners. Use when the component should read as rigid, precise, or grid-locked — code blocks, data tables, horizontal rules, and structural layout elements. The default aesthetic for this system; rounding is applied intentionally, not by default. Referenced by: unmapped.

sm.$description: Subtle rounding. Barely perceptible — softens a component without departing from the sharp aesthetic. Use for inputs, small tags, and elements where a hint of friendliness is appropriate but the component still reads as structured. Referenced by: unmapped.

md.$description: Standard rounding. Visibly rounded without feeling soft. Use for buttons, dropdown menus, select controls, and most interactive UI elements. The default rounding for actionable components. Referenced by: unmapped.

lg.$description: Comfortable rounding. Use for cards, panels, and contained content regions. Reads as a finished surface rather than a raw container. Referenced by: unmapped.

xl.$description: Prominent rounding. Use for modals, hero cards, and large featured surfaces where the rounding is a deliberate design choice rather than a subtle softening. Do not overuse — it competes with the system's sharp-corner default. Referenced by: unmapped.

full.$description: Pill or circle. Always fully rounded regardless of element size. Use for status badges, avatar circles, tag chips, toggle switches, and decorative pills. The value 9999px ensures a perfect pill at any width or height. Referenced by: unmapped.
